fault_hypothesis: The expiry check on line 73 uses a strict greater-than comparison (`>`), but it should be greater-than-or-equal (`>=`). This causes entries to be incorrectly returned as valid when the current time is exactly equal to the expiry time.

constraints.intent: The check at the boundary should handle equality, but the current code does not, so current time must not exactly equal effective expiry.
If an entry is considered valid (i.e., the expiry check fails), the current time must be strictly less than its effective expiry.
The effective expiry time, which accounts for clock skew, must be greater than or equal to the stored expiry time.
